join_order.notes: mmap p_name.bin + p_partkey.bin. Build uint8_t green_part[2000001] using 64 threads in parallel: split 2M parts into 64 equal chunks, each thread scans its chunk with strstr('green') and writes green_part[p_partkey[i]]=1. Safe: p_partkey is PK so all write indices are unique. Eliminates single-threaded 107MB fread bottleneck.

Build uint8_t year_by_orderrow[15000001] in parallel using 64 threads: each thread scans its chunk of o_orderdate (already mmap'd), computes year=extract_year(o_orderdate[row])-1992 as uint8_t, stores at year_by_orderrow[row]. Cost: scan 57MB o_orderdate (already mmap'd) in parallel — ~0.1ms wall-clock. Hot path: orow=orders_by_orderkey[l_orderkey[i]] (1 DRAM miss into 240MB), year_off=(int)year_by_orderrow[orow] (L3 hit: 15MB). Eliminates o_orderdate DRAM miss vs iter_0. Key difference from iter_1: indexed by ORDER ROW (15MB fits L3) not by orderkey (60MB exceeds 44MB LLC).
Precompute int32_t suppkey_to_nationrow[100001]: load supplier_by_suppkey + s_nationkey + nation_by_nationkey + n_name. 400KB, always L3-resident. Built single-threaded (trivial size).

Use pre-built partsupp_hash_index (mmap, 256MB). No runtime build cost. Composite key = partkey*200001ULL+suppkey, FIB_CONST=11400714819323198485ULL, LOG2_CAP=24. Deliberately kept as pre-built to avoid iter_1 mistake of adding ~121MB build I/O overhead that cost more than it saved.
25-row nation table loaded at startup. Folded into suppkey_to_nationrow. Trivial cost.

data_structures.green_part_filter: uint8_t[2000001], 2MB, L3-resident; indexed by p_partkey; built in parallel via mmap
data_structures.year_by_orderrow: uint8_t[15000001], 15MB, L3-resident; indexed by order row index (orow = orders_by_orderkey[l_orderkey]); stores (year-1992) as uint8_t; built in parallel from mmap'd o_orderdate; fits in 44MB L3 alongside other hot structures
data_structures.orders_by_orderkey: mmap int32_t[60000001], 240MB; MADV_RANDOM; first-level lookup orow = orders_by_orderkey[l_orderkey[i]]
data_structures.partsupp_ht: mmap pre-built partsupp_hash_index HTSlot[16777216], 256MB; MADV_RANDOM; no runtime build
data_structures.ps_supplycost: mmap double[8000000], 61MB; MADV_RANDOM; fetched via partsupp_ht row_idx
data_structures.aggregation: double profits[25][10] per thread in alignas(64) struct; reduced single-threaded after parallel phase
data_structures.l3_hot_budget: green_part(2MB) + year_by_orderrow(15MB) + suppkey_to_nationrow(0.4MB) = 17.4MB total hot structures, within 44MB LLC

parallelism.parallel_phase_1: dim_filter: launch nthreads threads; each scans chunk of p_name (for green_part build) and chunk of o_orderdate (for year_by_orderrow build); use std::barrier or join+relaunch for phase synchronization
parallelism.parallel_phase_2: main_scan: morsel-driven over 60M lineitem rows with atomic morsel counter
parallelism.thread_local_state: double profits[25][10] per thread in alignas(64) struct
parallelism.reduction: single-threaded sum of thread_profits after parallel scan

indexes.purpose: O(1) filter lineitem by l_partkey; 2MB L3-resident; built in parallel
O(1) orow → year_offset (0-6); 15MB L3-resident; replaces o_orderdate DRAM miss with L3 hit
O(1) l_orderkey → orow; 240MB; 1 DRAM miss per qualifying row (unavoidable first-level lookup)
O(1) avg (l_partkey, l_suppkey) → ps_supplycost row; 256MB; DRAM random access; kept pre-built to avoid build overhead

aggregation_detail.structure: double profits[25][10] indexed by [nationrow][year-1992], in alignas(64) ThreadProfits struct per thread
aggregation_detail.finalize: iterate 25x10, collect non-zero (n_name, year, sum) tuples, sort by (nation ASC, year DESC)

hot_path_per_qualifying_lineitem_row: int32_t orow = orders_by_orderkey[l_orderkey[i]];  // 1 DRAM miss into 240MB (prefetched)
int year_off = (int)year_by_orderrow[orow];  // L3 hit into 15MB (was: DRAM miss into 57MB + compute)
double sc = lookup_supplycost(l_partkey[i], l_suppkey[i], ps_ht, ps_supplycost);  // DRAM (256MB + 61MB)
optimization_notes: Root cause: two bottlenecks identified. (1) Category A — plan restructuring: the dim_filter phase in iter_0 runs entirely single-threaded, doing a 107MB fread of p_name.bin followed by a serial strstr scan of 2M parts while all 64 worker threads sit idle; this costs ~13ms of sequential preprocessing. Fix: use mmap for p_name.bin and p_partkey.bin, then launch all nthreads threads to scan p_name in parallel (safe since p_partkey is a PK, all write indices to green_part[] are unique). Also build year_by_orderrow in parallel during the same phase. (2) Category C — work elimination: the orders lookup chain has a two-level DRAM access (orders_by_orderkey[l_orderkey[i]] -> o_orderdate[orow] -> extract_year), costing 2 DRAM misses per qualifying row. Fix: precompute uint8_t year_by_orderrow[15000001] indexed by order row — 15MB fits in 44MB LLC (unlike iter_1's 60MB okey_yearoff indexed by orderkey which exceeded LLC). Total L3 hot budget: green_part(2MB)+year_by_orderrow(15MB)+suppkey_to_nationrow(0.4MB)=17.4MB, well within 44MB. The pre-built partsupp_hash_index is kept as-is (no runtime build) to avoid iter_1's mistake of adding ~121MB build I/O overhead that exceeded the savings. Software prefetch for orders_by_orderkey added to hide the one remaining DRAM latency chain.
